spec_match.rationale: Lays out all six tokens with correct camelCase spellings and a before/after surgical-logout panel that nails the spec's motivation. Misses the spec rule that selecting `cache` or `*` auto-appends `prefetchCache` to the cleared set — `*` correctly nukes everything in this demo but `cache` alone does not flip the prefetch row.

interactive.rationale: Click any token tile to flip it on/off; the header builds live and the impact matrix recolours. Well past static-card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises all six tokens, an impact matrix, and a pre-v138 vs v138 logout comparison — the page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Wildcard, individual, and combined token selections are all live, but cross-origin scoping, active-prerender skip, and the implicit cache→prefetchCache promotion are all absent.

references.rationale: The see-also chromestatus link points to feature 5114014507040768, which is a different feature. The correct ID for this concept is 4755745652080640 (per the parent feature index). Invariant #3 violation — the self-heal script will misroute this page on cleanup.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables only, click-toggle pattern uses the proper inverse (background var(--text-black) with color var(--bg-ivory)), no static-card.

openQuestions.title: Fix the wrong chromestatus feature ID in see-also
openQuestions.detail: The see-also link is to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5114014507040768 but this page is about feature 4755745652080640. The wrong ID will break the slug-recovery script in /tmp/fix-slugs.py (per CLAUDE.md) — it reads the id out of the page and could rename the folder to the wrong slug. Replace with 4755745652080640.

openQuestions.title: Reflect the `cache` → `prefetchCache` promotion in the impact matrix
openQuestions.detail: Per spec, selecting `cache` (or `*`) implicitly appends `prefetchCache` to the cleared list. Right now ticking `cache` alone leaves the `prefetch cache` cell as `kept`, which is wrong. Either expand the `map` JS object so `cache` also marks `pf`, or render a small footnote `(implicit: also clears prefetchCache)`.

openQuestions.title: Drop the unused `.token.new::after { content: "v138"; position: absolute; }` rule
openQuestions.detail: The pseudo-element has `position: absolute` with no positioning context defined and is currently invisible — the `v138 NEW` text comes from the `<small>` tag. Either remove it or finish wiring it (top: 4px; right: 4px; background: var(--accent-blue); etc.) so the tiles get a corner badge.

openQuestions.title: Add an `executionContexts` token
openQuestions.detail: The header is described as having `six tokens` in the lede but only five are shown (cookies / storage / cache / prefetchCache / prerenderCache / *). The csd-prefetch sibling page includes `executionContexts`. Either add it here or correct the lede to say five.

summary: Beautiful comparison-driven page that frames the motivation well, but the chromestatus link is wrong (major — breaks the slug-recovery automation) and the `cache` → `prefetchCache` promotion is missing. Fix those two and the page becomes a strong teaching reference.
